definition: Rare aircraft are uncommon, noteworthy, or chase-worthy sightings for plane spotters. Rarity is contextual and can come from low production numbers, end-of-life status, limited operators, special features, geographic infrequency, historical value, or a notable individual airframe.

factors.label: Unique design, special livery, or notable frame
Rare visitor for the local area or airport
Historical, iconic, endangered, or high-wow aircraft

not_universally_rare: Everyday A320-family aircraft at major hubs unless special-livery, unusual-operator, geographically unusual, or otherwise notable.
Everyday 737-family aircraft at major hubs unless special-livery, unusual-operator, geographically unusual, or otherwise notable.
Normal Boeing 747-400 or 747-8 cargo flow, including routine Kalitta-style traffic, unless special-livery, notable-registration, emergency, unusual-operator, rare-route, or otherwise notable.
